ops.text: Verifying guard-pack changes locally is a minefield of silently-wrong harnesses: (1) `uv run --project ../captain-hook` runs the sibling checkout whose path override pins UNRELEASED cc-transcript v8, whose bash parser folds string-embedded substitutions (`echo "$(curl …)"`) that the RELEASED parser surfaces span-less — every empirical parser probe this session made was v8-flavored, and two inline test rows written from those probes broke CI (fixed forward in abe83002); the CI mirror is `uv run --no-project --with capt-hook`. (2) `capt-hook --hooks plugin/hooks run` silently loads the registered plugin-cache pack ALONGSIDE the tree — discovered via CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR and the user-level HOME config, so old-pack denials/rewrites masquerade as tree behavior; full isolation needs `env -u CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR -u CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR HOME=<scratch>` plus a manifest-free copy of the hooks dir (the `[pack] name="ccx"` manifest summons the cached pack by name). Neither trap produces any warning.
